name: La Tarte Tatin
recipeIngredient: 1/2 lb Puff pastry (fresh or thawed frozen)
5 lbs Golden Delicious apples (peeled, cored, quartered and tossed with Lemon juice)
recipeInstructions:
FOR PASTRY: Preheat oven to 300°F. Roll pastry to 1/8 inch thickness. Cut into a circle 1 inch larger than the diameter of a 1-quart soufflé dish. Transfer to a baking sheet and prick with fork. Bake for 30 minutes. If not quite browned, increase heat to 350°F. Combine 1/2 cup sugar with water in an 8-inch skillet and cook, swirling pan frequently, until caramelized. Pour into the 1-quart soufflé dish.
Begin adding apples, arranging vertically (fill dish as compactly as possible since fruit will shrink during baking). Lay remaining apples on top.
Sprinkle with remaining 1/2 cup sugar and cinnamon. Pour melted butter evenly over top. Cover with foil, making several slits to allow steam to escape. Place a piece of foil on the lower oven rack to catch any juices that might overflow. Bake, basting frequently, until apples are tender, about 1-1/2 hours.
Remove from oven and cool for 15 minutes. Carefully pour off juices into an 8-inch skillet and boil over medium-high heat until reduced and caramelized.
Run a knife around the inside of the soufflé dish to loosen apples. Set pastry over fruit. Place a serving plate on top and invert. Pour caramelized juices over top. Heat a heavy skillet until quite hot. Dust tarte with sugar and hold bottom of hot skillet over top of tarte, searing sugar, or place under broiler. Repeat 3 more times.
Serve warm or cold.
